title: Restore real OCR.space extraction flow and enforce consistent purple theme (v18 app unchanged otherwise)

requirements.summary: Replace the OCR upload offline placeholder with a real OCR.space HTTPS extraction flow and continue into existing /ocr/review parsing + approval UI.
acceptanceCriteria: On /ocr/upload, the app can extract text from a user-selected supported file (image/PDF) via OCR.space over HTTPS (no manual “paste text” requirement to proceed).
The “Offline Mode: This app cannot perform real OCR extraction…” messaging is removed/replaced with accurate user-facing copy describing the real OCR process.

When OCR extraction succeeds, the extracted text is saved to the OCR session storage and the user is navigated to /ocr/review with parsed questions populated (using the existing text-to-questions parsing logic).
When OCR extraction fails (network/API error, unsupported file, quota exceeded), the user sees a clear error message and can retry or choose a different file without the rest of the app breaking.
No other routes/screens/features outside the OCR flow are removed or altered.

file_operations.description: Add a small OCR.space client wrapper that accepts an image/PDF File, performs HTTPS multipart upload to OCR.space, and returns extracted text plus any user-displayable error details (handle common failure cases like network errors, bad file types, and quota/limit responses).

Replace the manual “paste text”/mock extraction flow with a real OCR.space extraction flow: validate supported file types, call the new OCR.space client, show loading/progress UI, show clear retryable errors, and on success save the session and navigate to /ocr/review. Use existing shadcn-ui components (Button, Alert, Card, Textarea/Label where still needed for display) for consistent UI; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Update the OCR session utility to store sessions created from real OCR results while preserving existing session load/clear/convert APIs used by /ocr/review.
acceptanceCriteria: The OCR review page (/ocr/review) continues to work without route changes and can render questions originating from real OCR extraction.
Session persistence still uses the existing localStorage key and supports clear/load behavior so cancel/back flows work as before.
Approved questions can still be inserted into the personal question bank exactly as before.

file_operations.description: Refactor the OCR session utility so it represents real OCR sessions (extractedText + parsed questions) while keeping the same localStorage key and the same exported APIs used by /ocr/review (loadOCRSession, clearOCRSession, convertOCRQuestionsToPersonalQuestions, and save behavior). Keep any legacy/mock-named exports working (as aliases if needed) so existing imports continue to work without changing non-OCR features.

Adjust copy/error handling to reflect real OCR extraction (not manual pasted text), while continuing to load the session via the existing session utility and render/approve/edit/insert questions exactly as before. Use existing shadcn-ui components (Alert, Card, Button, Checkbox, Textarea) for messaging consistency; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Ensure purple theme tokens and button/active-state styling render consistently across the app in light and dark mode without layout/feature changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary actions (default buttons/active states) visibly use the purple primary color across all pages, including navigation and key CTAs.
Theme is consistent in both light and dark mode (no pages/components falling back to non-purple default styling).
No UI screens are removed or restructured as part of theme adjustments (token-level/styling-only changes).

file_operations.description: Audit and adjust global theme tokens (primary/secondary/accent/ring and related foreground tokens) to ensure purple is consistently used in both light and dark mode; keep changes token-level only to avoid layout restructuring.

Verify Tailwind color mappings continue to reference the theme tokens (primary/secondary/accent/ring/etc.) so shadcn-ui variants (especially Button default/outline/ghost active states) consistently render purple across the app; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
